title: System intent satisfaction: construction cheapness
scope: Governing intent: Make correct package-surface construction cheap by design so validation confirms rather than teaches shape.
goal: Assess whether package-owned artifacts are easy to construct correctly before validation runs.
non_goals: Do not add new generators or schemas from this review alone.
Do not treat all validation failures as design failures.

review_mode.review question: Does current tooling make correct artifact shape obvious enough that validation is mostly confirmation?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: create-review output, startup guidance, schema reference generation, recent validation behavior

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-planning create-review for each review skeleton; make check after implementation lane; rg template/schema/reference guidance
review_method.evidence sources: .agentic-workspace/planning/reviews/TEMPLATE.review.json; .agentic-workspace/planning/reviews/README.md; docs/reference/startup-context.md; make check behavior from previous lane

findings.title: Satisfaction is medium: scaffolds help, but validation still teaches some required details
findings.summary: The create-review command produced valid dated skeletons quickly, and schema/reference checks catch drift. However, recent work still hit validation to discover missing schema documentation and closeout field wording, showing construction is not yet entirely cheap by design.
findings.evidence: create-review created the nine review records with sortable filenames; previous implementation lane required make check to reveal stale startup reference docs; archive-plan required exact active_milestone and follow-on wording before closeout.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents may continue relying on validation failures as the authoring interface for package-owned surfaces, which costs tokens and weakens first-pass success.
findings.suggested action: Defer promotion until repeated in another lane; if repeated, improve generator defaults or preflight diagnostics for the specific surface that caused the failure.
findings.promotion trigger: Promoted by maintainer request to file actionable findings as issues.

recommendation.defer: Implementation remains inactive until the issue is selected for execution.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not create a broad construction-cheapness issue without repeated evidence.

retention.trigger: repeated validation-as-authoring friction confirms or disproves the concern
retention.proof surface: generator output, validation failures, and scaffold defaults

drift_log: 2026-05-08: Review created and filled from durable-intent doctrine pass.
